questions.stem: When a site manager reviews a new eligibility application, which field carries the highest trust for determining the agreed verification workflow?
options: The application's description field, which summarizes the household situation
The Acceptance Criteria field, which documents the agreed verification workflow
The Release Notes Internal field, which carries the most recent updates
The system-generated status field, which reflects current processing state
questions.source_quote: Acceptance Criteria — the agreed workflow. This is what the guide should reflect.
questions.explanation: Acceptance Criteria is always the highest-trust field for the agreed workflow. Description is the lowest-trust source.

questions.stem: A site manager needs to assign a new track to their staff for the upcoming school year. Which status must a track have before it is visible to learners on their Learn home?
questions.source_quote: published — only published tracks appear on learner home screens
questions.explanation: Only published tracks appear on learner home screens. Draft tracks are visible to trainers only.

questions.stem: When a district director downloads the compliance report for their site, what best describes the data source in the demo environment?
options: Live data pulled directly from SchoolCafé in real time
Seeded demo district data — real district data arrives via the integration phase
Data exported from the Jira ticket system
A cached snapshot updated every 24 hours
questions.source_quote: The seeded roster is demo scenery; your real data arrives via the integration phase.
questions.explanation: The compliance report in demo mode uses seeded data. Real district data requires the roster-sync integration.
